essence: Helps people heal, grow, and understand themselves through deep listening, emotional insight, and evidence‑based therapeutic practice.

identity.title: Psychotherapist
identity.summary: Provides talking therapies to help individuals understand emotions, resolve inner conflicts, change patterns, and improve mental wellbeing.

salary.notes: Private practice, specialist modalities, and senior clinical roles can exceed £80k–£100k+. Income varies significantly depending on caseload and setting.

qualifications: Strong Understanding of Psychological Theory and Therapeutic Modalities
Ongoing Professional Development and Personal Therapy

lifestyle.description: Psychotherapists work in a calm, reflective environment where emotional presence and deep listening are central. Days involve one‑to‑one or group sessions, case notes, supervision, and ongoing study of therapeutic approaches. The work is emotionally intense — clients bring trauma, anxiety, relationship struggles, and long‑held patterns — and therapists must maintain strong boundaries and self‑care. Progress can be gradual, but the meaning is profound: helping someone understand themselves, break cycles, or find stability can be life‑changing. This lifestyle suits individuals who are empathetic, patient, grounded, and committed to long‑term personal and professional growth.
